sections.paragraph: (“taking hold of the heel, supplanter, layer of snares,” comp. Gen 25:26 27:36 Hos 12:4), pr.n. Jacob, the younger of the twin sons of Isaac; also called Israel (יִשְׂרָאֵל); the ancestor of the nation of Israel, -Gen. 2550 . אֱלֹהֵי יַעֲקֹב the God of Jacob, i.e. Jehovah, Isa 2:3 Psa 20:2. בֵּית יַעֲקֹב and simply יַעֲקֹב the house or family of Jacob; poetically used of the people of Israel, i.q. יִשְׂרָאֵל, בְּנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל compare עֵשָׂו. Used of the land of Israel, Gen 49:7 elsewhere used of the whole people regarded as one person, e.g. Isa 41:1-29, 43 , 44 , 45 , etc.; rarely used of the kingdom of Ephraim, Hos 12:3 Mic 1:5 Isa 17:4 or even, as also Israel is used in the later books, of the kingdom of Judah, Oba 1:18 Nah 2:3.
